reasons: Codex and ClaudeCode produced identical outputs, demonstrating independent convergence on the same results
Both agents successfully created the required volatility_top5.txt file with proper format
All validation criteria met: 5 records, valid GEM codes (300xxx), valid volatility percentages (0-100), descending order
Higher volatility values (15-37%) are more plausible for ChiNext stocks during a 10-day window in May 2024
Two-agent consensus provides stronger evidence than a single divergent result
GA failed to produce any output file, eliminating it from consideration
OpenClaw produced valid output but with significantly lower volatility values (9-13%), suggesting potential data collection issues despite superior code quality
merge_notes: Codex and ClaudeCode outputs are identical. Selected Codex as preferred agent (could equally be ClaudeCode). OpenClaw's implementation shows excellent engineering practices (retry logic, parallel processing, error handling) but the divergent results with lower volatility values suggest data quality or API access issues. The consensus between Codex and ClaudeCode on higher volatility stocks provides more confidence in correctness. GA did not complete the task.
